entities.name: mavacamten
entities.context: Long-term effect of mavacamten in obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy

entities.name: MAVA-LTE
entities.context: MAVA-LTE (NCT03723655) is an ongoing, 5-year, open-label extension study

entities.name: EXPLORER-HCM
entities.context: Participants from EXPLORER-HCM (NCT03470545) could enrol in MAVA-LTE

entities.name: left ventricular outflow tract gradient
entities.context: Sustained reductions from baseline to Week 180 occurred in left ventricular outflow tract gradients

entities.name: N-terminal pro B-type natriuretic peptide
entities.context: N-terminal pro B-type natriuretic peptide [median (interquartile range): -562

entities.name: left ventricular ejection fraction
entities.context: Mean left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) decreased from 73.9% (baseline) to 66.6%

entities.name: New York Heart Association class
entities.context: 77.9%) of the 95 patients improved by at least one New York Heart Association class from baseline

entities.name: reduced left ventricular ejection fraction
entities.context: 20 patients (8.7%; exposure-adjusted incidence: 2.77/100 patient-years) experienced 22 transient reductions in LVEF to <50%

entities.name: cardiac myosin
entities.context: mavacamten is a cardiac myosin inhibitor (implicit from HCM indication)

relations.evidence: Long-term mavacamten treatment resulted in sustained improvements in cardiac function and symptoms in patients with obstructive HCM
MAVA-LTE (NCT03723655) is an ongoing, 5-year, open-label extension study designed to evaluate the long-term effects of mavacamten
Participants from EXPLORER-HCM (NCT03470545) could enrol in MAVA-LTE
20 patients (8.7%) experienced 22 transient reductions in LVEF to <50% resulting in temporary treatment interruption
mavacamten is a cardiac myosin inhibitor used in HCM
NT-proBNP tracked as disease severity biomarker; sustained reductions from baseline
